page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.14xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It features 56 dedicated physical CPU cores with 56 vCPUs, operating at a ratio of one thread per core. The instance is configured with 480.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.57 GB per core.

This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The absence of hyperthreading means each vCPU maps directly to a dedicated physical core, offering predictable compute performance free from resource sharing at the core level.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density relative to the compute allocation positions this instance for memory-bound workloads rather than raw compute-heavy or storage-heavy tasks. The lack of bundled local storage and GPU accelerators represents a clear tradeoff, optimizing the hardware profile for memory-intensive applications such as large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise resource planning systems that do not require local disk performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.14xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It delivers 56 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 56 physical cores, operating with one thread per core to ensure predictable compute performance. The instance is equipped with 480.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.57 GB. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. This hardware profile is designed for memory-intensive workloads, such as in-memory databases, caching systems, and enterprise applications, where high memory capacity and dedicated core execution are prioritized over local disk I/O or graphics acceleration.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring fifty-six dedicated physical cores and four hundred and eighty gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.

480.0 GB of system memory providing 8.57 GB per core
